admission_type: open access (free access subject to a mandatory verification of personal preparation)

academic_requirements.summary: Free-access (non-restricted) master's programme. Admission requires a recognised Bachelor's degree with the naval/industrial-engineering curricular credits below, B2 English, and a mandatory verification of personal preparation. There is no selection ranking; candidates either meet a grade-based automatic exemption or must pass a pass/fail oral interview.
academic_requirements.prior_degree_or_diploma: A Bachelor's degree of at least 180 CFU/ECTS (e.g. in Naval Engineering or Nautical/Pleasure-Craft Engineering), or an equivalent foreign qualification recognised as suitable.
academic_requirements.required_prior_credits: At least 40 CFU (or equivalent knowledge) in the basic-science scientific sectors prescribed for Class L-9 (Industrial Engineering) degrees, plus at least 45 CFU (or equivalent knowledge) in characterising naval-engineering disciplines (e.g. Naval Architecture, Statics of the Ship, Ship Structures, Ship Propulsion Plants).
academic_requirements.subject_requirements: General knowledge of naval-engineering topics including naval architecture, shipbuilding and naval installations; assessed at the readiness interview.
academic_requirements.gpa_threshold: Candidates whose Bachelor's final mark is at least 9/10 of the maximum (or ECTS grade 'A'/'B'; reported as roughly >= 99/110) are deemed adequate and exempted from the interview. Holders of the UniGe first-level degrees in Naval Architecture and Marine Engineering or Pleasure Craft Engineering with that grade are exempt from the curricular check.
academic_requirements.international_qualification: Non-EU candidates residing abroad with a foreign qualification apply through the UniGeApply (Apply UniGe) portal for pre-evaluation, then complete Universitaly pre-enrolment and obtain a study visa via the Italian Embassy/Consulate.

accepted_proofs: An English-language certification recognised by the University at level B2 or higher (CLAT list, https://clat.unige.it/en/CertificazioniRiconosciute).
The University B2 English test (separate assessment dates published; registration closes about 10 days before each test date).
A degree taught entirely in English, certified officially.
exemption_rules: Holding a certification recognised by the University demonstrating B2 English bypasses the language test.
language_requirements.assessment_or_upload_stage: English proficiency is verified at the readiness stage; candidates without an accepted certificate sit the University's B2 test (further details via the Polytechnic School website).

entry_exam_or_test.test_name: Verification of personal preparation (assessment of adequacy of personal readiness) - an oral interview before a three-member committee. NOT a TOLC.
entry_exam_or_test.applies_to: Applicants who do not qualify for the grade-based automatic exemption (Bachelor's final mark below 9/10 of the maximum, reported as below ~99/110).
entry_exam_or_test.format: Oral interview conducted by a three-member committee to ascertain the student's general knowledge of naval-engineering topics (naval architecture, shipbuilding, naval installations). A separate B2 English test applies to candidates without an accepted English certificate.
entry_exam_or_test.dates: [uncertain] The page lists the prior-year (2025/2026) interview sessions (29 September 2025, 17 November 2025, 19 January 2026, 27 February 2026); explicit A.Y. 2026/2027 dates were not confirmed on the fetched page and are expected to follow the same autumn-to-February pattern.
entry_exam_or_test.scoring_or_threshold: Outcome is recorded as 'passed' or 'failed' only; there is no numerical ranking. A pass (or the grade-based exemption) is required for enrolment. Pre-registration and the test are free of charge.

rounds: Spring session for EU citizens and non-EU residents in Italy (UniGeApply): opens 9 April 2026; master's degrees close 28 August 2026.
Pre-registration (master's) on the UniGe portal: from 9 April to 30 November 2026 (until 10 March 2027 with late payment).
application_deadline_eu.enrolment_deadline: Confirmation of enrolment from 15 July 2026 to 30 November 2026 (until 10 March 2027 with late payment). Enrolment in years after the first runs 15 July to 5 October 2026, with arrears accepted 6 October to 5 November 2026.
application_deadline_eu.notes: This is a free-access (open) programme, so EU/resident applicants are not ranked; the personal-preparation interview is pass/fail. The programme deadlines page lists the generic UniGe enrolment calendar; the institution-wide spring UniGeApply window applies to non-EU residents in Italy.

rounds: Fall session for non-EU candidates requiring a visa (UniGeApply): opens 26 November 2025; institution-wide close 20 March 2026; pre-evaluation results by 22 May 2026.
application_deadline_non_eu.visa_or_pre_enrolment_deadline: After receiving eligibility via UniGeApply, visa applicants complete Universitaly pre-enrolment and then apply for the study visa at the Italian Embassy/Consulate (with diploma translation and Declaration of Value).
application_deadline_non_eu.early_closure: None published for this programme. The known fall-session early closures for A.Y. 2026/27 (Medical-Pharmaceutical Biotechnology 13 Feb 2026, Maritime Science and Technology 18 Feb 2026, Computer Engineering bachelor 8 Mar 2026) do NOT include Yacht Design.
application_deadline_non_eu.notes: Non-EU visa applicants must use the fall session; the UniGeApply fall window for A.Y. 2026/27 (closed 20 March 2026) has already passed as of the research date (10 June 2026). Yacht Design appears on the UniGeApply English master's non-EU selection list.

required_documents.document_name: Valid passport (non-EU) or national identity card (EU)
required_documents.notes: Passport is the only document accepted for non-EU applicants on UniGeApply; EU applicants may use a national identity card.

required_documents.document_name: Bachelor's degree certificate (final or provisional) and transcript of records
required_documents.notes: Uploaded on UniGeApply for pre-evaluation of the naval/industrial-engineering qualification and curricular credits.
Listed among UniGeApply submissions for master's pre-evaluation.

required_documents.document_name: English language proficiency certificate (B2 or higher) or Medium of Instruction (MOI) certificate
required_documents.notes: Required to satisfy the B2 English requirement; candidates without an accepted proof sit the University's B2 test.

required_documents.document_name: Diploma translation and Declaration of Value (or CIMEA Statement of Comparability) and legalization of the foreign qualification
required_documents.notes: Required for non-EU visa applicants with a foreign qualification to finalise enrolment; obtained via the Italian Embassy/Consulate or CIMEA.

required_documents.document_name: Universitaly pre-enrolment submission and study-visa evidence
required_documents.notes: Visa applicants complete Universitaly pre-enrolment after UniGeApply eligibility, then apply for the study visa.

source_quotes.quote: Yacht Design, course code 11958, Degree Class LM-34 (Naval Engineering), Teaching Language English, Campus La Spezia, 120 CFU, 2 years
This degree course is free access, but requires passing a test.

a Bachelor degree (of at least 180 CFU/ECTS) ... at least 40 CFU, or equivalent knowledge, in scientific sectors prescribed by Class L-9 (Industrial Engineering) ... at least 45 CFU in naval engineering disciplines including Naval Architecture, Static of the Ship, Ship Structures, Ship Propulsion Plants.
a Bachelor's degree grade is at least 9/10 of the maximum or has obtained a final grade corresponding to the 'A' or 'B' classification of the ECTS system ... admission test exemption.

The assessment consists of an oral interview conducted by a three-member committee ... aims to ascertain the student's general knowledge focusing on Naval Engineering topics including naval architecture, shipbuilding, and naval installations.
Those with a certification recognised by the university demonstrating B2 English proficiency bypass the language test.

uncertainty_notes: The programme overview page states 'free access, but requires passing a test'; the requisites summary phrased access as 'restricted'. Reconciled as free access subject to a mandatory pass/fail readiness verification (with grade-based exemption) rather than a ranked restricted-access selection.

Interview session dates on the requisites page are for A.Y. 2025/2026 (Sep 2025-Feb 2026); A.Y. 2026/2027 dates were not confirmed on the fetched page and are expected to follow the same pattern. Confirm on the Polytechnic School website.
The corsi.unige.it deadlines page lists the generic UniGe enrolment calendar; the institution-wide UniGeApply spring (residents) and fall (visa) windows were used for the application windows.
No programme-specific fall-session early closure is published for Yacht Design for A.Y. 2026/27.
Programme is delivered at the La Spezia campus.
